data.title: Quantum Firm Infleqtion Seeks $540 Million Listing, Targets Both Computing and Sensors
data.description:
Louisville-based quantum computing company, Infleqtion, plans to go public through a $540 million deal with Churchill Capital Corp X. The companies filed paperwork with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on January 6, 2026. Once the deal closes, the new company will be named Infleqtion, Inc. and trade under the ticker -INFQ.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The deal is expected to bring in more than $125 million in new capital from investors. As a result, Infleqtion would become the first public company focused on neutral-atom quantum systems. This move gives public market investors direct access to a fast-growing part of the quantum space.

Quantum Progress with Real World Uses

Infleqtion works in both quantum computing and precision sensing, which helps spread risk across markets. On the computing side, the company recently reached 12 logical qubits with error control. This result beat its own 2026 target and supports a longer goal of 1,000 logical qubits by 2030.

At the same time, Infleqtion is working with Nvidia (NVDA) to connect quantum systems with AI tools. This work aims to let quantum hardware fit into current data systems. As a result, the company can move faster toward practical use.

Beyond computing, Infleqtion also builds quantum sensors. Recently, it showed a quantum clock that can guide underwater vehicles where GPS does not work. In addition, the company holds a U.S. Army contract for secure navigation and works with Safran Electronics and Defense (SAFRY) on timing tools set for early 2026.

Overall, Infleqtion enters the market with early revenue paths, strong partners, and clear goals.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to line up notable publicly traded companies on Wall Street. It's a quick way to see how they stack up and where the field could be heading.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: My Size Stock (MYSZ) Soars on AI Fashion Data Monetization Plans
data.description:
My Size (MYSZ) stock was on the rise Tuesday after the mobile device measurement solutions company announced plans to explore the monetization of its data intelligence assets. The company collects anonymized data from consumers who use its artificial intelligence (AI) to test apparel fits on e-commerce platforms before purchases. Now, it's looking to offer its insights into sizing, mismatches, and other fashion fit data to brands and retailers.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

My Size said its efforts include evaluating the development of a secure Data Intelligence Framework that will feature usage-based digital access models, such as data credits. With this platform, the company could sell insights into consumer habits while remaining compliant with data protection regulations, including GDPR and CCPA.

Ronen Luzon, CEO of My Size, said, "We believe that aggregated data intelligence can play an increasingly important role in helping the fashion industry reduce inefficiencies and improve decision-making. Our focus is on exploring ways to responsibly unlock the value of these insights while maintaining the highest standards of consumer privacy, security, and regulatory compliance."

My Size Stock Movement Today

My Size stock was up 18.87% in pre-market trading on Tuesday, following a 14.9% rally yesterday. The stock has also rallied 24.3% year-to-date but was down 63.9% over the past 12 months.

Today's rally came with increased investor interest in MYSZ stock, as some 800,000 shares changed hands. For comparison, the company's three-month daily average trading volume is roughly half that at 435,000 units.
data.author: William White

data.title: Dow Jones Muted on Steady CPI Inflation Data
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is set to open Tuesday slightly in the red as investors digest new inflation data.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

The Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) announced the Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose by 2.7% year-over-year, matching the estimate of 2.7% and unchanged from November. Furthermore, the core Consumer Price Index, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, increased by 2.6% year-over-year, below the estimate of 2.7% and holding steady from the prior month.

data.title: IBM Expands Cloud Access to New Quantum Chips as It Pushes Deeper Computing
data.description:
International Business Machines Corporation (IBM) has opened early access to two new quantum processors, giving customers a closer look at how the company plans to scale quantum computing. The move focuses on running larger programs while keeping errors low, which remains one of the biggest limits in the field.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the center of the update is a new 120-qubit processor called Nighthawk. At the same time, IBM also expanded access to an upgraded Heron system that improves reliability. Together, the systems show IBM's focus on steady progress rather than chasing the highest qubit count.

Customers on IBM's Premium and Flex cloud plans can now access the first Nighthawk system, known as ibm_miami. According to IBM, this processor features the most advanced design the company has released to date.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: AlphaTON Capital Stock (ATON) Rockets on Nvidia AI Plans
data.description:
AlphaTON Capital (ATON) stock experienced a massive rally on Tuesday after the public technology company confirmed a $46 million artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure deal. While this news was confirmed on Monday, investors didn't latch onto the stock until after markets closed.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

AlphaTON Capital noted that this new AI infrastructure deal will see it add 576 of Nvidia's (NVDA) B300 chips, making up a half cluster, to its operations. The company said this deployment will be ready next month and will bring a 27% internal rate of return, 282% return on investment, and a net present value of $11 million. This will also mark the company's first large-scale confidential compute deployment.

Brittany Kaiser, CEO of AlphaTON, said, "We are contributing confidential compute power to the world's largest privacy-preserving AI network. Cocoon provides compute that isn't owned or monitored by Big Tech. A privacy-first network where users maintain sovereignty over their data. This is not a philosophical position, it's a trillion-dollar market requirement that AlphaTON is uniquely positioned to capture."

AlphaTON Capital Stock Movement Today

AlphaTON Capital stock was up 146.15% in pre-market trading on Tuesday, following a 1.04% rally yesterday. The shares have rallied 51.67% year-to-date but were still down 78.38% over the past 12 months.

ATON stock experienced heavy trading this morning, as some 34 million shares changed hands, compared to a three-month daily average of about 1.13 million units.
data.author: William White

data.title: Investors Sue Bitdeer Technologies (BTDR) Over Mining Chip Claims
data.description:
A class action lawsuit was filed against Bitdeer Technologies Group (BTDR) on December 4, 2025.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The plaintiffs (investors) in the federal securities class action allege that they bought Bitdeer stock at artificially inflated prices between June 6, 2024 and November 10, 2025. They are now seeking compensation for financial losses incurred upon public revelation of the company's alleged misconduct during that time.

Investors who bought Bitdeer stock during that period can click here to learn about joining the lawsuit.

Bitdeer is a self-described world-leading technology company for Bitcoin mining and AI cloud. Bitdeer says it "handles complex processes involved in computing."  These include equipment procurement, transport logistics, datacenter design and construction, equipment management, and daily operations. Bitdeer also claims that it offers forward-thinking cloud capabilities to customers with high demand for artificial intelligence. 

Although its headquarters are in Singapore, Bitdeer has deployed Bitcoin mining and HPC datacenters globally. In particular, it says it has major operations not only  in the United States, but also in Bhutan, Norway, Canada, Malaysia, and Ethiopia.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Why Is Beyond Air Stock (XAIR) Up Today?
data.description:
Beyond Air (XAIR) stock rocketed higher on Tuesday after the company announced a deal between one of its subsidiaries and XTL Biopharmaceuticals (XLTB). Under the terms of this agreement, XTL Biopharmaceuticals has agreed to acquire 85% of NeuroNOS, a biotechnology company focused on the development of disease-modifying therapeutics targeting the core pathophysiology of Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) and neuro-oncology.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

XTL Biopharmaceuticals has taken an interest in NeuroNOS due to the growing number of children diagnosed with ASD. Current estimates are that roughly one in 31 children in the U.S. are affected by ASD, which is a major increase and has sparked government concern. Even so, there's no FDA-approved treatment for the underlying neurobiological mechanisms of ASD.

NeuroNOS' work to treat ASD has already resulted in two Orphan Drug Designations from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA). These cover treatments for Phelan-McDermid Syndrome, a rare genetic disorder with strong autism correlation, and Glioblastoma, an incredibly aggressive and deadly form of brain cancer.

Beyond Air Stock Movement Today

Beyond Air stock was up 27.85% in pre-market trading on Tuesday, following a slight drop yesterday. The shares have rallied 20.66% year-to-date but have fallen 87.83% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of XAIR stock, as some 28 million shares changed hands, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of 6.04 million units.

Investors will also note that XTL Biopharmaceuticals stock surged 15.87% this morning alongside increased trading activity.
data.author: William White

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Trend Lower, All Eyes on Inflation Report, Bank Earnings
data.description:
U.S. stock futures moved lower on Tuesday morning as traders awaited the key Consumer Price Index (CPI) reading for December 2025 and results from large U.S. banks for the fourth quarter of 2025. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.21%, 0.12%, and 0.21%, respectively, at 8:17 a.m. EST on January 13.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Monday's regular trading, all three major indexes closed in the green, with the S&P 500 rising 0.16%, the Dow gaining 0.17%, and the Nasdaq Composite advancing 0.26%.

Looking ahead, investors will closely watch today's CPI data to gain a greater understanding of inflationary trends and fill gaps left by last fall's extended U.S. government shutdown. Dow Jones consensus estimate indicates a 2.7% price rise over the twelve months through December. This figure aligns with November's lower-than-expected CPI data.

Turning to trending stocks, JPMorgan Chase (JPM) reported better-than-expected Q4 2025 earnings, driven by strength in the trading business. Shares of Intel (INTC) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) rose 3.4% and 2.7% in Tuesday's pre-market trading after KeyBanc upgraded both chipmakers to Buy, citing memory cycle and robust data center demand.

Meanwhile, Delta Air Lines (DAL) stock fell about 4% after reporting mixed Q4 2025 results. In contrast, shares of L3Harris Technologies (LHX) jumped more than 12% in pre-market trading after the defense contractor announced that it would pursue an initial public offering (IPO) of its missile solutions business in the second half of 2026 through a partnership with the U.S. Defense Department.
